---
title: "Sample Title"
author: "Brian Linton"
format: pdf
date: today
params:
  species: "My species"
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

Example using a parameter: 
The native range of `r params$species` spans from…

Example italicizing a parameter: 
The native range of *`r params$species`* spans from…

Example of NEFSC stock assessment report citation in .bib format:

@TechReport{NEFSC_2020_MTA_FALL,
%author = "{Northeast Fisheries Science Center}",
author = "NEFSC",
shortauthor = "NEFSC",
title = "Fall Management Track Assessments 2020",
institution = "US Dept Commer, Northeast Fisheries Science Center",
% address ="166 Water Street, Woods Hole, MA 02543-1026",
number="22-08",
%type={Center Reference Document},
type={Ref. Doc.},
note="173p.",
month="",
pages = "1--173",
year = "2022",
eprinttype={noaair},
eprint={39404}
% url="https://repository.library.noaa.gov/view/noaa/39404"
}

Example of how to move up two directory levels in a relative pathname:

```{r}
my_data <- read.csv(here:here("../../extra_data/red_fish.csv"))
```

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
